default_bullets.text: Meet with Target's officers and management in order to develop an understanding of operations, including its:

children.text: Basis of financial information;
Organization structure;
Historical growth by geography; and
Finance function, financial reporting framework and internal control environment.

default_bullets.text: Read Target's financial statements and discuss Target's accounting policies and practices with management, including:

children.text: Finance function, financial reporting framework, and management reporting relationships (including processes, timing, structure of financial reporting, and interaction with the information/accounting systems);
Significant accounting policies and recent or contemplated changes in accounting principles, procedures, or estimates;
Significant accounting estimates (e.g. bad debt provision, rebates, capitalized R&D, etc.); and
Intercompany accounts and related party transactions, if any.

default_bullets.text: Analyze the accounting policies as disclosed in the audited financial statements and perform a high-level gap assessment to identify differences between the Target’s accounting policies and practices and IFRS Accounting Standards as applied by Parent.
Consider if the following accounting policies have been applied consistently within and across historical periods:

children.text: Cash versus accrual accounting policies;
Capitalization policies;
Depreciation policies; and
Differences between interim and year-end procedures; and
Revenue recognition policies;

default_bullets.text: Identify, and where possible quantify, potential earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization ("EBITDA") normalization items by considering:

children.text: The impact of the normalization adjustments identified by Management;
Large, unusual or non-recurring events, or transactions that may have distorted results;
The impact of any changes to senior management or management team structure;
The impact of provisions (allowance for doubtful accounts, inventory obsolescence, other), management estimates or adjusting entries on the reported results;
The impact of foreign currency transactions;
The impact of related party transactions and shared services provided by other entities, if applicable;
The impact of any changes in accounting policies, procedures and estimates; and
Other potential items identified during the due diligence process.

default_bullets.text: Obtain a comprehensive view of revenue and margins. Obtain the underlying transactional data (i.e. sales database), if available, and gain an understanding of the major trends in revenue and margins addressing areas including:

children.text: Revenue and margin by significant customer and end market/ segment/ region;
Price and volume trends.
impact of large or long-term orders/ seasonality (if applicable); and
any non-recurring components of revenue.
customer category (i.e. individual, business);
distribution channel (i.e. online, wholesale, etc.); and
price, volume, mix and cross-sell analysis.
Seasonal fluctuations in demand and monthly sales trends; and

default_bullets.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target's revenue and inquire about:

children.text: Gross to net revenue;
Revenue recognition policies;
Revenue and gross margin by customer;
Organic growth and delineation between price vs. volume;
Pricing practices;
Promotions and discounts;
Any non-recurring components of revenue;
Revenue seasonality, if any;
Other components of cost of sales and key suppliers.

default_bullets.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target’s expenses and inquire about:

children.text: Costing methodology;
Cost of sale trends, impact of material change in vendors (if any);
Employee compensation and related costs by function;
Selling, general, and administrative expenses;
Repairs and maintenance expense;
Unusual and extraordinary items (if any).

default_bullets.text: Obtain monthly details of the Company's consolidated working capital and analyze and comment on:

children.text: The composition of individual working capital accounts (e.g., trade receivables, accounts payable and accruals, etc.);
Monthly working capital trends, metrics, and seasonality;
Large, unusual, or non-operating items that may have affected normal working capital trends, (such as accruals for bonuses, capital accruals, month end vs quarter end differences, etc.); and
Understanding the accounting impact of allowance for uncollectible amounts and other accruals requiring judgment.

default_bullets.text: Obtain and read an analysis of the Target’s accounts receivable and inquire and comment on:

children.text: Billed/unbilled aging analysis, turnover and days sales outstanding;
Credit terms;
Trade and non-trade balances;
Allowance for uncollectible accounts and write-offs.

default_bullets.text: Obtain and read an analysis of accounts payable, accrued liabilities and customer deposits and inquire about:

children.text: Accounts payable aging and days outstanding;
Accrued liabilities;
Supplier settlement terms; and
Other current and non-current liabilities.

default_bullets.text: Obtain and read an analysis of existing and future capital cost requirements including:

children.text: Maintenance versus growth capital expenditures;
Other historical, deferred, and planned capital expenditures

default_bullets.text: Inquire about significant commitments and contingent liabilities including:

children.text: Pending or threatened litigation or investigations by regulatory or other authorities; and
Contractual obligations, including leases;
Purchase commitments; and
Employee agreements (e.g. transaction/retention bonuses, change-in-control, deferred compensation, or severance agreements, etc.);
Incentive compensation and employee benefit obligations;
Expected or contingent liabilities (e.g. environmental, litigation, regulatory and tax); and
Other off-balance sheet transactions.

default_bullets.text: Summarize and comment on net debt items (on and off-balance sheet) presented by Management and other potential debt-like items;

Consider whether elements of working capital have the nature of and may be reclassified as net debt, and if so, summarize the potential impact of these adjustments on working capital and net debt; and

financial_due_diligence.text: The deliverable will be in the form of a report and Excel data book.

Unless otherwise noted, our work will concentrate on financial information for the last three fiscal years ended the most recent interim period, along with the most recent available financial information (e.g. year-to-date the most recent interim period or more recent monthly data as available).

quality_of_earnings.text: Analyze historical earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (“EBITDA”) at segment levels including understanding the impact of potential non-recurring or non-operation items such as:
Obtain and comment on the Seller’s proposed adjustments analysis;
Working with Client, understand potential impact with potential change in incentive compensation, dis-synergies, or synergies (if applicable);
Understand the impact of the Company’s JV business in the dredging sector;
The impact of significant accounting policies, provisions, management estimates or adjusting entries on the reported results;
Other large, unusual or non-recurring events or transactions that may have distorted results;
Non-cash items such as the impact of provisions, management estimates, or adjusting entries on the reported results; and
Other potential items discovered in the due diligence process.

revenue_analysis.text: To the extent available, obtain and read an analysis of Target’s revenues and margins and inquire about the following:
Analyze revenue, margin, pricing and average project length on a project basis by the following (where available):

children.text: Revenue stream (civil work, special projects (buildings), services and equipment rental);
Customer
Geography
Industry

revenue_analysis.text: Understand Target’s job costing methodology (components included in margin, and periodic true ups)

Understand Target’s revenue recognition and billing process (e.g. percentage of completion, periodic and contract completion true-ups (and its variability), loss provision process, policies in place to control accounting recognition and policy escalations, and lifecycle), with specific focus on Construction Management contracts, and its corresponding timing impact to EBITDA;
Understanding the profile (and exposure) of major project/contracts (i.e. Indigenous Partnership with Target.);
Obtain and analyze the backlog and active backlog as at latest available date (backlog margin on a project basis, and analysis of historical backlog conversion)

working_capital.text: Obtain and read an analysis of working capital and inquire about the following on a combined basis:
Understand the composition of individual working capital accounts to determine sufficient working capital needs of the Company (including bonding requirements);
Monthly trending and fluctuations in working capital and impact on reported metrics (e.g. days sales/payables/inventory outstanding, net working capital as a percentage of revenues, etc.);

Large, unusual, or non-operating items and current or upcoming initiatives that may affect normalized working capital trends, such as exited business, settlements, capital projects, understanding non-trade balances, etc.
Gain an understanding of the nature of the Company’s unearned revenue.

prepaid_materials_and_other_assets.text: Prepare a summary of other assets (including prepaid materials, prepaid assets) and comment on unusual items, significant fluctuations and significant balances

inventory.text: Prepare a summary of inventory and obtain an understanding of the Company’s inventory costing methodology, tracking of inventory, work-in-progress and obsolescence reserve.

accounts_payable_and_accrued_liabilities.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target’s accounts payable and accrued liabilities and inquire about:

children.text: Terms of trade with major suppliers, significant vendor allowances, rebates and/or purchase commitments;
Trade payables aging analysis and days payable outstanding analysis;
Billings in excess of cost profile; and

capital_expenditure_requirements.text: Obtain an understanding of the Company’s current equipment, including:

children.text: Mix of owned versus leased equipment/ vehicles;
Average age of equipment/ vehicles;
For leased fleet, understand the annual payment requirements;
For owned fleet, obtain details of historical capital expenditures and comment on planned, committed, and deferred capital expenditures.

net_debt.text: Prepare a summary of debt and debt-like items (e.g. notes payable, letters of credit, capital vendor payables, deferred revenue, pension deficit, warranty matters, exposure from onerous contracts, contingent matters, etc.), other assets, and liabilities and comment on unusual items, significant fluctuations and significant balances.
Summary of lease right-of-use assets and liabilities.

related_parties.text: Inquire about Target’s transactions (e.g. income statement and balance sheet impacts) with related parties including subsidiaries, holding companies, joint ventures, variable interest entities, and other entities under common ownership.

commitments_and_contingencies.text: Obtain and comment on any contingent claims or liabilities including:

children.text: Pending or threatened litigation or investigation by regulatory or other authorities; and
Other off-balance sheet transactions (including contingencies and guarantees).

accounting_overview.text: Understand and summarize the financial statement preparation process, including:

children.text: Finance function, financial reporting framework, and management reporting relationships (including process, timing, structure of financial reporting, and interaction with the information/ accounting systems);
Integration of recent acquisitions and status of any ongoing integration processes;
Significant accounts estimates and any recent or contemplated changes in accounting principles, procedures, or estimates.
If available, understand the reviewed statements unrecorded differences, management letter points, and other significant findings encountered during the annual review.
Target’s accounting policies and the key differences with IFRS Accounting Standards as applied by Parent

(Following the high-level gap assessment, if a more detailed gap assessment is required, we will work with Client to identify topics for further analysis and the estimate fees for the incremental analysis.)
